summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a special meeting to address zoning relief measures for residents affected by the July 6, 2022 tornado. The board adopted an emergency resolution directing staff on zoning provisions, creating a new application form for tornado-damaged properties, waiving fees, allowing temporary RV/camper placement during rebuilding, protecting nonconforming uses, and implementing a temporary moratorium on enforcement of certain zoning provisions. The meeting lasted approximately 25 minutes.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda passed unanimously

Resolution R-041-2022 adopted unanimously: directing staff on zoning relief for tornado-affected properties, authorizing new application forms, implementing temporary moratorium on enforcement of certain zoning provisions, waiving fees, and declaring an emergency
Motion to adjourn at 7:25 PM passed unanimously
action_items: Staff to help tornado-affected property owners with site plans using GIS capabilities
Staff to contact known tornado-damaged property owners to inform them of relief measures
Staff to post resolution information on Facebook and other public notification sites
Zoning/building department to implement new permit numbering system with 'T' suffix for tornado-related applications
key_amounts: Zoning fees waived for construction and reconstruction of structures destroyed by act of God (tornado)
notable_topics: Zoning relief for July 6, 2022 tornado recovery
Waiver of zoning permit fees for tornado-damaged properties
Temporary allowance for RVs/campers on properties during rebuilding
Protection of nonconforming uses to allow rebuilding
New tornado-specific zoning application form and numbering system (GT-2022-XXX-T)
Expedited application processing for tornado-affected residents
Site plan requirements and setback compliance
County coordination on building permit fees
attendees: Victor Eckler (mentioned, did legwork on zoning resolution)
Commissioner Painter (mentioned, assisted with county permit fee clarification)
notes: The transcript has significant portions of garbled/repeated text likely due to automated transcription errors. The meeting was called in response to a tornado that struck Goshen Township on July 6, 2022. Commissioner Painter clarified that county building permit fees would not apply to rebuilding even if the new structure has a different footprint than the original. A new permit numbering system was implemented with a 'T' suffix to flag tornado-related applications for the building department.
